title: Parahyparrhenia annua

The classification of Parahyparrhenia annua is further defined by its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system.

intro: Parahyparrhenia annua is an annual grass plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Parahyparrhenia annua include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions.
